evals.name: dbt-with-context
evals.prompt:
Simulate a full project-planning session. The user says:

"I want to add YouTube watch history to my analytics. I have CSV exports from Google Takeout and want to see monthly viewing patterns by channel and category."

Simulate the interview by providing reasonable user responses:
- When asked about data source: "The CSVs have video title, channel name, watch timestamp, and video URL. I'll manually export and drop them in a folder monthly."
- When asked about existing models: "No YouTube models exist yet, but I want it to follow the same pattern as media_activity."
- When asked about dimensions: "Channel, video category, and day of week. I want to see what days I watch the most."
- When asked about aggregations: "Daily and monthly summaries."

Follow the project-planning skill fully: enter plan mode, check for existing work, conduct the interview, create the plan document, then present for approval. Save the plan to the correct folder.
evals.expected_output: Should: 1) Enter plan mode, 2) Check existing dbt models, 3) Recognize problem/success already provided in initial prompt, 4) Ask follow-up questions about data structure, 5) Detect dbt project type, 6) Create comprehensive plan at project-plans/dbt/youtube-watch-history.md

assertions.name: checked_existing_work
assertions.description: Searched for existing dbt models or plans

assertions.name: recognized_context
assertions.description: Did not re-ask problem/success questions since they were in initial prompt

assertions.name: plan_has_sections
assertions.description: Plan includes: Discovery Summary, Scope & Goals, Technical Approach, Risk Assessment, Task Breakdown, Expected Output

evals.name: n8n-minimal-context
evals.prompt:
Simulate a full project-planning session. The user says:

"I need a workflow for backing up my Plex database"

Simulate the interview by providing reasonable user responses:
- When asked about the problem: "My Plex database has gotten corrupted twice and I lost watch history. I need automated backups."
- When asked about success criteria: "Daily backups stored on my NAS, kept for 2 weeks, and I get notified if a backup fails."
- When asked about trigger: "Daily at 3am when nothing is streaming."
- When asked about failure handling: "Send a Discord notification to my alerts channel."
- When asked about existing workflows: "There's a media-related workflow but it doesn't do Plex backups."

Follow the project-planning skill fully: enter plan mode, query n8n MCP for existing workflows, conduct the interview, create the plan document. Save to correct folder.
evals.expected_output: Should: 1) Enter plan mode, 2) Query n8n MCP (list_workflows, get_workflow_stats), 3) Ask core questions since minimal context, 4) Detect n8n project type, 5) Ask about triggers/failure handling, 6) Create plan at project-plans/n8n/plex-database-backup.md

assertions.name: queried_n8n_mcp
assertions.description: Used mcp__n8n-manager tools to check existing workflows

assertions.name: asked_core_questions
assertions.description: Asked about problem and success criteria

evals.name: docker-ambiguous
evals.prompt:
Simulate a full project-planning session. The user says:

"Let's plan adding Jellyfin to the homelab"

Simulate the interview by providing reasonable user responses:
- When asked about the problem: "I want an alternative to Plex that's fully open source. Some family members prefer Jellyfin's interface."
- When asked about success criteria: "Jellyfin running alongside Plex, pointing to the same media library, accessible via Tailscale."
- When asked about volumes: "Same media paths as Plex - C:/media/movies and C:/media/tv. Config in the jellyfin service folder."
- When asked about ports: "Use 8096 for web UI."
- When asked about networking: "Needs to be on the default bridge network, no special requirements."
- When asked about secrets: "No API keys needed for basic setup."

Follow the project-planning skill fully: enter plan mode, check existing Docker services, conduct interview, create plan. Save to correct folder.
evals.expected_output: Should: 1) Enter plan mode, 2) Check existing Docker services and compose files, 3) Ask core questions, 4) Detect Docker project type, 5) Ask about volumes/ports/networks/secrets, 6) Create plan at project-plans/docker/jellyfin-media-server.md

assertions.name: checked_existing_services
assertions.description: Checked existing docker-compose files or running containers

assertions.name: asked_core_questions
assertions.description: Asked about problem and success criteria

assertions.name: asked_docker_specifics
assertions.description: Asked about volumes, ports, networks, secrets
